Package org.eventb.core.seqprover.proofBuilder

Class ProofBuilder

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.proofBuilder.ProofBuilder
public class ProofBuilder extends Object
This class contains static methods that can be used to reconstruct proof tree nodes from proof skeleton nodes.

There are currently three ways in which this is supported:

  • Reuse : The simplest and most efficient, but also the most quick to fail way to reconstruct a proof tree node from a proof skeleton node. Only the rule stored in the proof skeleton node is tried for reuse. No reasoner is called. This way is guaranteed to succeed if the sequent of the open proof tree node satisfies the dependencies of the proof skeleton. The only exception to the above statements is when a reasoner version has changed: in this case, the reuse fails.
  • Replay : Each reasoner that is mentioned in the proof skeleton in replayed to generate a rule to use. The rules present in the proof skeleton nodes are ignored. This method can be used to re-validate proof skeletons since it completely ignores stored rules.
  • Rebuild : A mixture between reuse and replay. Reuse is performed when possible, and if not, replay is tried. In addition, replay hints are taken into account and generated to be used in subsequent replays in order to support refactoring.

The methods in this class should not be used directly, but should be used in their tactic-wrapped form.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • IProofSkeleton
  • ProofTreeNode
  • BasicTactics

    • ProofBuilder

  • Method Details

    • getReplayOutput

      private static IReasonerOutput getReplayOutput(IProofRule rule, IProverSequent sequent, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)

    • getTranslatedSkeleton

      private static IProofSkeleton getTranslatedSkeleton(IProofTreeNode node, IProofSkeleton skeleton)

    • reuse

      public static boolean reuse(IProofTreeNode node, IProofSkeleton skeleton, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)
      A method that recursively constructs proof tree nodes only using the rules from a proof skeleton.

      No reasoners are called for this proof reconstruction method

      This method may be used for a quick way to reconstruct a proof from a proof skeleton when its proof dependencies are satisfied.

      Parameters:
      node - The open proof tree node where reuse should start. This node MUST be checked to be open before calling this method.
      skeleton - The proof skeleton to use
      proofMonitor - The proof monitor that monitors the progress of the replay activity
      Returns:
      true iff the all proof tree nodes could be completely rebuilt from the rules present in the proof skeleton nodes. If this method returns false, it (possibly) means that the proof skeleton of the constructed tree is not identical to the one provided since the proof skeleton could not be completely reused for this node. TODO : Test this code & wrap using appropriate tactic.

    • recReuse

      private static boolean recReuse(IProofTreeNode node, IProofSkeleton skeleton, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)

    • replay

      public static boolean replay(IProofTreeNode node, IProofSkeleton skeleton, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)
      A method that recursively constructs proof tree nodes using a proof skeleton and replay hints using only reasoner replay.

      This method can be used to re-validate proof skeletons since it completely ignores stored rules.

      Parameters:
      node - The open proof tree node where rebuilding should start. This node MUST be checked to be open before calling this method.
      skeleton - The proof skeleton to use
      proofMonitor - The proof monitor that monitors the progress of the rebuild activity
      Returns:
      true iff the all proof tree nodes could be completely rebuilt from the proof skeleton nodes using reasoner replay. If this method returns false, it (possibly) means that the proof skeleton of the constructed tree is not identical to the one provided since a reasoner either failed or was not installed. TODO : Test this code & wrap using appropriate tactic.

    • recReplay

      private static boolean recReplay(IProofTreeNode node, IProofSkeleton skeleton, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)

    • rebuild

      @Deprecated public static boolean rebuild(IProofTreeNode node, IProofSkeleton skeleton, ReplayHints replayHints, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)
      Deprecated.
      use rebuild(IProofTreeNode, IProofSkeleton, ReplayHints, boolean, IProofMonitor)
      A method that recursively rebuilds proof tree nodes using a proof skeleton and replay hints.

      If no replay hints are present, this method first tries to reuse the proof rules in the proof skeleton to rebuild proof nodes. If this fails, it resorts to replaying the reasoner to generate a new proof rule that to use.

      In case there are some replay hints, these hints are applied to the reasoner input and the reasoners are replayed.

      If this proof tree node was successfully rebuilt, new replay hints are generated and this method is recursively called.

      Parameters:
      node - The open proof tree node where rebuilding should start. This node MUST be checked to be open before calling this method.
      skeleton - The proof skeleton to use
      replayHints - The replay hints to use
      proofMonitor - The proof monitor that monitors the progress of the rebuild activity
      Returns:
      true iff the all proof tree nodes could be completely rebuilt from the proof skeleton nodes WITHOUT recalling any of the reasoners. If this method returns false, it (possibly) means that the proof skeleton of the constructed tree is not identical to the one provided since either:
      • The proof skeleton could not be completely rebuilt for this node.
      • Reasoners needed to be recalled, changing the rules present in the proof skeleton.

    • rebuild

      public static boolean rebuild(IProofTreeNode node, IProofSkeleton skeleton, ReplayHints replayHints, boolean tryReplayUncertain, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)
      A method that recursively rebuilds proof tree nodes using a proof skeleton and replay hints.

      If no replay hints are present, this method first tries to reuse the proof rules in the proof skeleton to rebuild proof nodes. If this fails, it resorts to replaying the reasoner to generate a new proof rule that to use.

      In case there are some replay hints, these hints are applied to the reasoner input and the reasoners are replayed.

      If rules have a IConfidence.UNCERTAIN_MAX confidence, the tryReplayUncertain parameter controls what this function does. If tryReplayUncertain is false, uncertain rules are reused as is; tryReplayUncertain is true, a replay is attempted. If it succeeds, then the replayed rule is applied. If it fails, then the uncertain rule is kept in the resulting proof tree (as if replay had not been attempted). In all cases, the rebuild goes on recursively with the subtree of the uncertain rule.

      If this proof tree node was successfully rebuilt, new replay hints are generated and this method is recursively called.

      Parameters:
      node - The open proof tree node where rebuilding should start. This node MUST be checked to be open before calling this method.
      skeleton - The proof skeleton to use
      replayHints - The replay hints to use, or null if none
      tryReplayUncertain - true to try to replay uncertain rules, false to reuse them as is
      proofMonitor - The proof monitor that monitors the progress of the rebuild activity
      Returns:
      true iff the all proof tree nodes could be completely rebuilt from the proof skeleton nodes WITHOUT recalling any of the reasoners. If this method returns false, it (possibly) means that the proof skeleton of the constructed tree is not identical to the one provided since either:
      • The proof skeleton could not be completely rebuilt for this node.
      • Reasoners needed to be recalled, changing the rules present in the proof skeleton.
      Since:
      3.1

    • recRebuild

      private static boolean recRebuild(IProofTreeNode node, IProofSkeleton skeleton, ReplayHints replayHints, boolean tryReplayUncertain, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)

    • tryReuse

      private static boolean tryReuse(IProofRule reuseProofRule, IProofTreeNode node, ReplayHints replayHints)

    • tryReplay

      private static boolean tryReplay(IProofRule reuseProofRule, IProofTreeNode node, ReplayHints replayHints, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)

    • tryUncertainRule

      private static boolean tryUncertainRule(IProofTreeNode node, IProofRule reuseProofRule)
      Try to apply the given uncertain rule.

      Lowers its confidence to uncertain if needed.

      Parameters:
      node - an open node
      reuseProofRule - an uncertain rule to apply
      Returns:
      true if rule application succeeded, false otherwise

    • ruleIsSkip

      private static boolean ruleIsSkip(IProofTreeNode node, IProofRule rule)
      Tells whether a rule would really modify the proof tree, rather than just producing exactly the same sequent as a subgoal. If the test succeeds, then the rule has exactly one antecedent and can safely be skipped.

    • tryCompatibleSubtree

      private static boolean tryCompatibleSubtree(IProofTreeNode node, IProofSkeleton skeleton, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)
      Try to apply a child of the given skeleton to the given node.

      Returns true in case of success, which means that the given node has been completely rebuilt from a subtree of the skeleton.

      In case of failure, the given node remains unchanged.

      Parameters:
      node - an open node
      skeleton - a skeleton to search for compatible subtrees
      proofMonitor - the proof monitor
      Returns:
      true if a compatible subtree has been found and applied, false otherwise

    • tryRebuildUnsorted

      private static boolean tryRebuildUnsorted(IProofTreeNode[] nodeChildren, IProofSkeleton skeleton, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)
      Try to rebuild the given unsorted children from those of the given skeleton.

      Returns true in case of success, which means that the given nodes have been completely rebuilt from the skeleton.

      Parameters:
      nodeChildren - unsorted nodes
      skeleton - a skeleton
      proofMonitor - the proof monitor
      Returns:
      true if rebuild succeeds, false otherwise

    • rebuild

      @Deprecated public static boolean rebuild(IProofTreeNode node, IProofSkeleton skeleton, IProofMonitor proofMonitor)
      Deprecated.
      use rebuild(IProofTreeNode, IProofSkeleton, ReplayHints, boolean, IProofMonitor)
      A method that recursively rebuilds proof tree nodes using a proof skeleton and no replay hints.

      This method calls the more general rebuild() method with initially empty replay hints.

      Parameters:
      node - The open proof tree node where rebuilding should start
      skeleton - The proof skeleton to use
      proofMonitor - The proof monitor that monitors the progress of the rebuild activity
      Returns:
      true iff the all proof tree nodes could be completely rebuilt from the proof skeleton nodes WITHOUT recalling any of the reasoners. If this method returns false, it (possibly) means that the proof skeleton of the constructed tree is not identical to the one provided since either:
      • The proof skeleton could not be completely rebuilt for this node.
      • Reasoners needed to be recalled, changing the rules present in the proof skeleton.
